author: Tester <t@t.com>
body:
## Findings

Surveyed `~/p/asterinas-workspace/issues/` end-to-end (968 issues,
122 directories, 16 phase/epic dirs read in full). Extracted distinct
header-field values via `grep -rhE "^\*\*<field>:\*\*"`. Surveyed
beads via `reference/beads/cmd/bd/`, `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md`,
`AGENTS.md`. Cross-referenced against `docs/storage-format.md` v2.5.

Key data shape: asterinas writes prose-tagged headers. `Status:` has
913 instances normalizing to 11 categorical roots dominated by `done`
(495), `not-started` (245), `in-progress` (71), with a long
parked/deferred/sketch/superseded tail. `Workflow:` (703 + Tag 57 +
Workflow tag 18 = 778 cases) collapses to three values:
`[host+qemu]` (538), `[silicon-only]` (242), `mixed` (~30).
`Blocked by:` / `Blocks:` (675/324) mix local-numeric refs, full ids,
and relative paths. Provenance edges (`Found by`/`Discovered by`/
`Surfaced by`/`Spawned by`/`Stems from`/`Split from`/`Related`,
combined 219 edges) all collapse to our existing `discovered-from` +
`related` kinds. Orthogonal classification axes (`Phase:` 75,
`Lane:` 39, `Track:` 42, `Milestone:` many) all map to labels.
Sizing (`Estimated session budget` 104 + `Effort` 37 = 141) labels
cleanly. Numeric `Priority:` (12 explicit) and `Severity:` (59)
labels cleanly.

Genuine gaps where existing primitives don't carry the load:
1. Full-text search across 968 issue bodies — `jq` + grep works but
   doesn't scale for daily orchestration. Beads ships `bd search`.
2. Staleness query — 71 InProgress issues at this scale needs
   `bd stale`-style sweep verb. The data exists (`updated_at`).

Audit at `docs/host-asterinas-audit.md:1` — 32-row mapping table +
ship/skip recommendation + 6 conventions-that-lose-info notes + open
questions for the migration ticket `cc2fa96`.

## Recommendation

Ship two soft-targets:
- `bc6b9d9` — `jjf search <query>` (full-text body/title/comments).
- `e726cde` — `jjf stale --days N`.

Both filed with `parent-child` edge to `ec5a0a8`. Neither carries a
`blocks` edge to `cc2fa96` (host-asterinas-migrate) — the migration
can proceed without them; they make orchestration cheaper after.
Lean-toward-skip held for everything else: numeric priority,
`Status::{Deferred,Parked,Superseded}`, time-based defer, severity
field, sizing field, extended issue-type enum, `bd lint`/`duplicates`/
`kv`/`audit`/`hooked`/`molecule`/`convoy`/`wisp`. All
asterinas-convention metadata that doesn't ship lands in labels.

## Confidence

high. Data is concrete (968 files actually surveyed, distinct values
counted), beads surface read against the actual `cmd/bd/` files, and
the calibration rule was applied with clear thresholds (>= 200 issues
need it AND no label workaround → ship). The 32-row mapping table is
auditable line-by-line against the asterinas tree. Slight uncertainty
on the parked/deferred/superseded call — 48 issues is the borderline
case; if the migration finds the label workaround painful in practice,
revisit and ship a richer status surface as a follow-up.

## Open follow-ups

- `bc6b9d9` — `jjf search` (filed, parent-child to `ec5a0a8`).
- `e726cde` — `jjf stale --days N` (filed, parent-child to `ec5a0a8`).
- The migration ticket `cc2fa96` needs a deterministic local-numeric
  → 7-hex id mapping table built during the directory walk plus a
  two-pass `Blocked by:` resolver. Recorded in the audit's "Open
  questions" section but NOT separately filed — it's scope of the
  migration ticket, not a new one.
- If the migration finds the parked/deferred/superseded labels
  painful in practice, file a follow-up to widen `Status` enum.
